subject: Chemistry

question_text: Naturally occurring boron consists of two isotopes whose atomic weighs are 10.01 and 11.01. The atomic weight of natural oron is 10.81. Calculate the percentage of each isotope in natural boron.

subject: Chemistry
question_text: Igniting MnO2 converts it quantitatively to Mn3O4. A sample of pyrolusite is of the following composition : MnO2 80%, SiO2 and other inert constituents 15%, rest being water. The sample is ignited in air to constant weight. What is the percentage of Mn in the ignited sample? [O = 16, Mn = 54.9]

subject: Chemistry
question_text: One gram of an alloy of aluminium  and magnetism when treated with excess of dil. HCl forms magnesium chloride, aluminium chloride and hydrogen. The evolved hydrogen, collected over mercury at $${0^o}C$$ has a volume of 1.20 litres at 0.92 atm. pressure. Calculate the composition of the alloy. [H = 1, Mg = 24, Al = 27]

subject: Mathematics
question_text: A triangle $$ABC$$ has sides $$AB=AC=5$$ cm and $$BC=6$$ cm Triangle $$A'B'C'$$ is the reflection of the triangle $$ABC$$ in a line parallel to $$AB$$ placed at a distance $$2$$ cm from $$AB$$, outside the triangle $$ABC$$. Triangle $$A''B''C''$$ is the reflection of the triangle $$A'B'C'$$ in a line parallel to $$BC$$ placed at a distance of $$2$$ cm from $$B'C'$$ outside the triangle $$A'B'C'$$. Find the distance between $$A$$ and $$A''$$.

subject: Mathematics
question_text: From a point $$O$$ inside a triangle $$ABC,$$ perpendiculars $$OD$$, $$OE, OF$$ are drawn to the sides $$BC, CA, AB$$ respectively.  Prove that the perpendiculars from $$A, B, C$$ to the sides $$EF, FD, DE$$ are concurrent.

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Balls are drawn one-by-one without replacement from a box containing $$2$$ black, $$4$$ white and $$3$$ red balls till all the balls are drawn. Find the probability that the balls drawn are in the order $$2$$ black, $$4$$ white and $$3$$ red.

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Find the derivative of $$\sin \left( {{x^2} + 1} \right)$$ with respect to $$x$$ first principle.

subject: Mathematics
question_text: If x = a + b, y = a$$\gamma $$  + b$$\beta $$ and z = a$$\beta $$ +b$$\gamma $$ where $$\gamma $$ and $$\beta $$  are the complex cube roots of unity, show that xyz = $${a^3} + {b^3}$$.

question_text: Find the equation of the circle whose radius is 5 and which touches the circle $${x^2}\, + \,{y^2}\, - \,2x\,\, - 4y\, - 20 = 0\,$$ at the point (5, 5).

question_text:
Sketch the solution set of the following system of inequalities: 
$$${x^2} + {y^2} - 2x \ge 0;\,\,3x - y - 12 \le 0;\,\,y - x \le 0;\,\,y \ge 0.$$$

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Solve for $$x:\,\sqrt {x + 1}  - \sqrt {x - 1}  = 1.$$

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Solve the following equation for $$x:\,\,2\,{\log _x}a + {\log _{ax}}a + 3\,\,{\log _{{a^2}x}}\,a = 0,a > 0$$

subject: Mathematics
question_text:
If $$\left( {m\,,\,n} \right) = {{\left( {1 - {x^m}} \right)\left( {1 - {x^{m - 1}}} \right).......\left( {1 - {x^{m - n + 1}}} \right)} \over {\left( {1 - x} \right)\left( {1 - {x^2}} \right).........\left( {1 - {x^n}} \right)}}$$ 
where $$m$$ and $$n$$ are positive integers $$\left( {n \le m} \right),$$ show that 
$$\left( {m,n + 1} \right) = \left( {m - 1,\,n + 1} \right) + {x^{m - n - 1}}\left( {m - 1,n} \right).$$

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Show that the square of $$\,{{\sqrt {26 - 15\sqrt 3 } } \over {5\sqrt 2  - \sqrt {38 + 5\sqrt 3 } }}$$ is a rational number.

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Find all integers $$x$$ for which $$\left( {5x - 1} \right) < {\left( {x + 1} \right)^2} < \left( {7x - 3} \right).$$

subject: Mathematics
question_text: If $$\tan \alpha  = {m \over {m + 1}}\,$$ and $$\tan \beta  = {2 \over {2m + 1}},$$  find the possible values of $$\left( {\alpha  + \beta } \right).$$

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Express $${1 \over {1 - \cos \,\theta  + 2i\sin \theta }}$$ in the form x + iy.

subject: Physics
question_text: A horizontal uniform rope of length L, resting on a frictionless horizontal surface, is pulled at one end by force F. What is the tension in the rope at a distance $$l$$ from the end where the force is applied?

subject: Physics
question_text:
A car accelerates from rest at a constant rate $$\alpha $$ for some time after which it decelerates at a constant rate $$\beta $$ to come to rest. If the total time lapse is t seconds, evaluate.
(i) maximum velocity reached, and
(ii) the total distance travelled.
